message:
fix(ft-kernel-cpu): log_softmax precision via (x-max)-log(sum) reordering [bd-ebrb] + softmax conformance [bd-fntr]

Surfaced by torch_softmax_scipy_subprocess_conformance during the
/reality-check pass: at large_positive_1d=[1000,1001,1002],
FrankenTorch returned -2.4076059644444285 vs scipy's
-2.4076059644443806 — diff ~5e-14 (~1000 ULPs), well above the
libm noise floor.

Root cause: log_softmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 (and the f32
variant) computed
    log_sum_exp = max + log(sum(exp(x - max)))
    output[i] = x[i] - log_sum_exp
which evaluates as x[i] - (max + log(sum_exp)). For x ~ 1000 and
max ~ 1000, the intermediate sum is ~1000-magnitude but the result
is ~ -O(1) — the subtraction triggers catastrophic cancellation
that wipes out ~13 mantissa digits.

Fix: rewrite as
    log_sum_exp = log(sum(exp(x - max)))
    output[i] = (x[i] - max) - log_sum_exp
which is algebraically identical but keeps both intermediates near
the result magnitude. scipy.special.log_softmax uses this form for
the same reason. Applied to both f64 and f32 paths in
crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs.

Also adds torch_softmax_scipy_subprocess_conformance (closing
frankentorch-fntr) covering 9 cases: interior, uniform input,
scalar, large-positive / large-negative (where max-subtract
numerical stability matters), mixed-sign, and 1-D / 2-D-along-dim0 /
2-D-along-dim1 / 3-D-middle-dim shapes. 8-ULP tolerance with
1e-15 absolute floor for both softmax and log_softmax.

ft-kernel-cpu lib tests: 317 passing.
ft-api lib tests: 1136 passing.

Closes frankentorch-ebrb. Closes frankentorch-fntr (DB sync
drift — closure note in this message; JSONL is authoritative).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankentorch-35e","title":"Update FEATURE_PARITY.md to honestly track unimplemented PyTorch subsystems","description":"FEATURE_PARITY.md currently marks 'Full PyTorch drop-in surface: parity_green' but omits major PyTorch subsystems from the tracking matrix entirely, creating a misleading picture.

What to add:
1. New section: 'Deferred Subsystems (not yet in scope)' with explicit not_started status for: GPU/CUDA backend, Distributed training (DDP/FSDP), JIT/TorchScript/torch.compile, Quantization, Sparse tensors, Mixed precision (AMP), ONNX export, Gradient checkpointing, Custom autograd Functions (torch.autograd.Function).
2. For each: document why it's deferred (e.g., 'CPU-first vertical slice, GPU planned for Phase 3+'), link to any future closure beads.
3. Change 'Full PyTorch drop-in surface: parity_green' to accurately reflect that it covers the CPU eager-mode subset only.
4. Add a 'Parity Scope Declaration' section that explicitly states what IS and IS NOT in scope for the current phase.

This is the single most important documentation fix — without it, the project appears done when it's at ~70% of PyTorch's core API surface.

Tests: N/A (documentation only). Verify by reading the updated file and confirming every major PyTorch subsystem is either tracked as green or explicitly listed as deferred.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:28.495902714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:04:43.553923709Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:04:43.553642487Z","close_reason":"Already completed by another agent: Parity Scope Declaration section added, Full PyTorch drop-in changed to parity_gap, Deferred Subsystems section with 9 entries and tracking beads, Gap Policy updated. Verified all referenced beads exist and tensor_apply_function claim is accurate.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-3cp","title":"Capture committed perf-SLO baseline artifact for G5","description":"Follow-up to frankentorch-vws.\n\nThe perf-SLO runner/gate now exists in ft-conformance, strict cargo gates pass, and we captured a verified single-sample full-profile measurement in artifacts/phase2c/performance/perf_slo_report_full_once.json plus PERF_SLO_SUMMARY_FULL_ONCE.md. The current result shows all five direct spec budgets failing and the three regression budgets remaining baseline_required without a committed baseline.\n\nRemaining work:\n1. Run the full perf-SLO profile with the intended statistical envelope (default 100 iterations, warmups as configured) on a suitable worker/host.\n2. Commit a stable baseline artifact at the canonical perf_slo baseline path for regression comparisons.\n3. Decide whether the single-sample full_once artifact should stay as an exploratory snapshot or be superseded by the committed baseline bundle.\n4. Hand the baseline path/contract to frankentorch-ioo so G5 can call run_perf_slos + check_perf_slos deterministically in CI.\n\nNotes:\n- rch exec validates runtime execution but does not automatically persist generated artifacts back into the local worktree across separate invocations, so baseline capture should use a workflow that intentionally preserves the produced artifact bundle.\n- Direct spec-budget failures from the single-sample full profile are already documented in artifacts/phase2c/performance/PERF_SLO_SUMMARY_FULL_ONCE.md.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:37.780725984Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T03:27:16.468792093Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T03:27:16.468370434Z","close_reason":"Normalized the canonical perf-SLO measurement/baseline paths, committed perf_slo_baseline_v1.json plus PERF_SLO_BASELINE_V1.md, and aligned runner/checker defaults so G5 has a stable baseline contract. Higher-iteration replacement baseline tracked separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-3u6","title":"Audit dispatch/kernel CPU edge cases","description":"Deep audit pass for ft-dispatch/ft-kernel-cpu execution flow; fix bugs/edge cases and add tests as needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-11T03:24:33.376039097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T03:44:53.435483764Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T03:44:53.435310637Z","close_reason":"Fixed missing f32 test import; validated checks/tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-3v6e","title":"Remaining ft-api ops with fail-loud autograd guards: stft, istft, affine_grid, grid_sample, complex/view_as_real/view_as_complex","description":"After closing frankentorch-1tax (statistical/special ops), 7 ops in ft-api still reject requires_grad=true inputs with hard errors instead of carrying gradients:

- stft (line 1248) — short-time Fourier transform; PyTorch's torch.stft supports backward via inverse FFT.
- istft (line 1411) — inverse short-time Fourier transform; symmetric to stft.
- stft window tensor (line 6733) — separate guard rejecting tracked window inputs.
- affine_grid (line 7774) — torch.nn.functional.affine_grid supports backward.
- grid_sample (line 7905) — torch.nn.functional.grid_sample supports backward.
- complex (line 994/1001) — torch.complex(re, im) supports backward through real and imaginary inputs (Wirtinger derivatives).
- view_as_real / view_as_complex (lines 1101, 1145) — complex view ops also support gradients in PyTorch.

These are not 'severed-autograd' — they're explicit guards that fail-loud, so users can't accidentally lose gradients silently. But they DO block training paths that would work in PyTorch (e.g., audio models using STFT, vision models using affine_grid + grid_sample for spatial transformer networks).

Each child needs its own fix bead. Children grouped by domain:
  Audio:   stft + istft + stft-window
  Vision:  affine_grid + grid_sample
  Complex: complex + view_as_real + view_as_complex (deferred — Wirtinger calculus is non-trivial; PyTorch's complex autograd took years to stabilize)

Priority for audio/vision (P2): real downstream training paths blocked. Priority for complex (P3): Wirtinger calculus is genuinely tricky and may need its own design doc first.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:21:35.728631229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:21:35.728631229Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-3xp","title":"Add functional adaptive pooling ops (avg_pool, max_pool)","description":"Add functional_adaptive_avg_pool1d/2d/3d to ft-api. Critical for classification models.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:10.976814151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:40.354782411Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:40.354530184Z","close_reason":"Implemented functional_adaptive_avg_pool1d/2d/3d. 5 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-3ye","title":"Add missing activation functions: SELU, Hardtanh, Softshrink, Hardshrink, Tanhshrink, Softsign, Softmin","description":"Add remaining PyTorch activation modules to ft-nn for full coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:33:20.306579607Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T17:45:14.131546621Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T17:45:14.131285396Z","close_reason":"Implemented SELU, Hardtanh, Softshrink, Hardshrink, Tanhshrink, Softsign, Softmin. 7 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-40i","title":"Fix f32 index_select/gather/scatter output dtype","description":"Audit: index_select/gather/scatter in ft-autograd always emit F64 tensors even when input dtype is F32, despite f32 CPU kernels existing. This silently upcasts and breaks dtype preservation. Fix by dispatching to f32 kernels and emitting F32 storage, add f32 forward tests in ft-api.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:18:13.533424703Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T12:25:26.015235117Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T12:25:26.014918859Z","close_reason":"Implemented f32 index_select/gather/scatter dispatch + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -76,10 +77,12 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-duf7","title":"tensor_xlog1py masks NaN from y when x==0 (scipy propagates)","description":"scipy.special.xlog1py(0.0, NaN) returns NaN — it short-circuits to 0 only when x==0 AND !isnan(y). FrankenTorch's tensor_xlog1py composes through where(x==0, 0, x*log1p(y)), so the where branch swallows NaN from y whenever x==0. This diverges from scipy/PyTorch on the (x=0, y=NaN) case. Fix: extend the mask to also exclude NaN y, e.g. where(x==0 & !isnan(y), 0, x*log1p(y)). Surfaced by torch_xlog1py_scipy_subprocess_conformance during the frankentorch-b5of work; current conformance test trims the failing case so the rest of the harness can land. Severity: low (NaN edge case, no observed downstream impact in tests), but breaks bit-exactness against scipy at the y=NaN boundary.","notes":"Fixed in this commit: tightened the where mask in tensor_xlog1py from (x == 0) to (x == 0 && !isnan(y)) by composing tensor_eq + tensor_isnan + tensor_sub + tensor_mul. Now scipy / torch / FrankenTorch all return NaN at (0, NaN) instead of FrankenTorch's prior 0. Re-enabled the (0.0, NaN) case in torch_xlog1py_scipy_subprocess_conformance and added xlog1py_propagates_nan_from_y_when_x_is_zero regression test in ft-api.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T21:45:39.280076032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:15:03.713554559Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T22:15:03.709924002Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-dwy","title":"Convert DenseTensor::storage() panic to Result return type","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found a single production panic!() in ft-core/src/lib.rs:971-974:

```rust
panic!(
    "called storage() on {:?} DenseTensor; use typed_storage() or contiguous_values_as_f64()",
    other.dtype()
)
```

This fires when `.storage()` is called on a non-F64 DenseTensor. Since FrankenTorch supports F32, F16, BF16, Bool, I32, I64, Complex64, Complex128 dtypes, this panic is reachable in normal usage — it is NOT a "should never happen" guard.

## Problem
A library crate should never panic on valid inputs. This violates the Deterministic Autograd Contract (DAC) principle of fail-closed behavior: panics are not catchable in a structured way and produce undefined recovery state.

## Plan
1. Change `fn storage(&self) -> &[f64]` to `fn storage(&self) -> Result<&[f64], DenseTensorError>` (or rename to `storage_f64()` and return `Result`)
2. Add a new `DenseTensorError::UnsupportedStorageAccess { dtype: DType }` variant if not already present
3. Update ALL callers of `.storage()` in ft-autograd, ft-api, ft-kernel-cpu, ft-dispatch to handle the Result
4. Run full workspace tests + clippy to verify

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero panic!() calls in non-test production code across all crates
- All callers propagate the error properly
- Existing tests still pass

## Risk
Medium refactor — `.storage()` may be called in many places. Use `ast-grep` to find all call sites before starting.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:22:22.849643284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:33:22.953274004Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T23:33:22.953021486Z","close_reason":"DenseTensor::storage() now returns Result with UnsupportedStorageAccess errors; updated ft-api call sites and verified workspace test/check/clippy via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-e6b","title":"Align planning docs with optional oracle mirror and current shipped layers","description":"Update PLAN_TO_PORT_PYTORCH_TO_RUST.md and FRANKENSQLITE_ADAPTATION_CROSSWALK.md so they describe legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch as the typical local mirror when present and reflect that nn/optim/data layers now exist in-tree.","notes":"Updated planning docs so the PyTorch oracle path is described as a typical local mirror instead of an always-present checkout, and refreshed the implementation/conformance status to mention the in-tree ft-nn/ft-optim/ft-data layers and current harness entrypoints.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:13:43.289814480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:14:14.034784063Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:14:14.034032030Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-ebrb","title":"log_softmax kernel loses ~1000 ULPs at large magnitudes due to operation ordering","description":"log_softmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 (and the f32 variant) computes
    log_sum_exp = max + log(sum(exp(x - max)))
    output[i] = x[i] - log_sum_exp
which evaluates as x[i] - (max + log(sum_exp)). When x ~ 1000, max ~ 1000, the intermediate max + log(sum_exp) is ~1000-ish but the final result is ~−2; the catastrophic cancellation between two ~1000-magnitude values discards ~13 digits of mantissa.

scipy.special.log_softmax is mathematically equivalent but algebraically rearranged:
    output[i] = (x[i] - max) - log(sum_exp)
This keeps the intermediate small (~ −2 for x near max) and preserves full f64 precision.

Surfaced by torch_softmax_scipy_subprocess_conformance during the /reality-check pass: at large_positive_1d=[1000,1001,1002], FrankenTorch returns -2.4076059644444285 vs scipy's -2.4076059644443806 — a diff of ~5e-14 (about 1000 ULPs), well above the libm noise floor.

Fix: rewrite the output loop to compute (x - max) - log(sum_exp). The fast path (inner_size == 1) and the general path both need the change. Affects log_softmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 in crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs:2134 and the f32 variant at line 5729.

Severity: medium — the precision gap matters for cross-entropy training with logits in extreme magnitudes (e.g., overconfident classifiers in late training). Most practical inputs stay within ~1e2 magnitude where the gap is negligible.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Rewrote log_softmax_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64 (and the f32 variant) to compute (x - max) - log(sum_exp) instead of x - (max + log(sum_exp)). The two are algebraically identical but the new form keeps the intermediate small (~result-magnitude) instead of ~max-magnitude, which preserves full f64/f32 precision through the subtraction. softmax conformance now passes large_positive_1d and large_negative_1d cases bit-for-bit with scipy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:24:16.966235103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:26:01.316782889Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T22:26:01.314283244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-eeh","title":"Fix ft-api clippy needless_range_loop in tensor_nll_loss","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T19:57:24.182262932Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T19:58:31.100006039Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T19:58:31.099756857Z","close_reason":"Fresh rch clippy rerun passed for ft-conformance path; no local ft-api fix was required after verification","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-etu","title":"Investigate and close next verified parity gap from FEATURE_PARITY","description":"No open beads remain. Use current source + FEATURE_PARITY + remote quality gates to identify one still-missing or broken FrankenTorch parity surface, implement it, verify it, and sync beads.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-01T20:33:53.342830285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T20:42:25.194397110Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T20:42:25.194152728Z","close_reason":"Added conformance coverage for cov/corrcoef/mode/quantile in tensor_advanced fixtures and runner; verified targeted ft-conformance test passes remotely; workspace check passes remotely; remaining fmt/clippy failures tracked separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-f6nn","title":"ft-nn PReLU and shrink activations sever autograd","description":"Reviewer finding in crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs after the recent activation-gradient repair wave. Several activation modules still read tensor values, compute in plain f64, and return session.tensor_variable(..., false), so a requires_grad=true input produces a fresh untracked output and tensor_backward cannot propagate gradients. Concrete sites: PReLU::forward lines 3576-3642 reads both input and its learnable weight via tensor_values and returns an untracked leaf, so both input gradients and the PReLU weight gradient are dropped; Softshrink lines 3918-3938, Hardshrink lines 3966-3978, Tanhshrink lines 3991-4000, Softsign lines 4014-4023, Softmin lines 4046-4057, and RReLU lines 4154-4168 have the same severed-input pattern. Existing tests for these modules are forward-only (for example prelu_default_forward/prelu_has_parameters/prelu_multichannel, softshrink_shrinks, tanhshrink_values, softsign_bounded, softmin_is_softmax_of_negated, rrelu_module_*), and the full ft-nn test suite still passes: rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn reported 508 passed on 2026-04-29. Repro by code inspection: create a requires_grad=true input, run PReLU::forward (or any listed activation), reduce with tensor_sum, then tensor_backward; tensor_gradient for input is absent because the forward output is a requires_grad=false leaf. For PReLU, tensor_gradient for self.weight is also absent even though parameters() exposes that learnable slope. Fix by composing each activation through existing autograd-aware primitives (e.g. PReLU via tensor_where/tensor_mul with broadcasted weight; shrink ops via tensor_where; Softmin via tensor_neg or 0-input subtraction then tensor_softmax; RReLU via leaky_relu-style composition) and add regression tests asserting input gradients, plus weight gradients for PReLU.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:09:37.892015821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:53:11.745564147Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T18:53:11.743783997Z","close_reason":"Composed all 7 listed activation modules through autograd-aware primitives: PReLU via tensor_reshape+tensor_expand+tensor_mul+tensor_where (now propagates to both input and learnable weight); Softshrink/Hardshrink via nested tensor_where + tensor_abs/tensor_sub/tensor_add + tensor_gt/tensor_lt; Tanhshrink via tensor_sub(input, tanh(input)); Softsign via tensor_div(input, 1+abs); Softmin via tensor_neg + tensor_softmax; RReLU via where(x>=0, x, slope*x). Added 8 gradient regression tests asserting analytical d/dx for each module (plus PReLU weight grads, both single-slope and multichannel). All 11 existing forward tests continue to pass — forward parity preserved.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-fntr","title":"softmax / log_softmax lack subprocess conformance against torch/numpy","description":"tensor_softmax and tensor_log_softmax are the canonical activation/output functions for classification heads, attention layers, and most loss formulations. They have unit tests in ft-api but no subprocess conformance against an authoritative reference (torch.nn.functional.softmax / scipy.special.softmax / numpy). Bit-exact agreement matters because:

- Numerical stability: log_softmax(x) = log(softmax(x)) is computed via the max-subtraction trick to avoid exp overflow; the equivalence with the naive form must hold within libm precision across all inputs.
- Gradient stability: softmax/log_softmax forward/backward are coupled (dL/dx = softmax(x) - target for cross-entropy). Drift between FrankenTorch and PyTorch here corrupts every classification training run.
- Edge cases: large positive inputs (exp overflow risk), large negative (underflow), uniform input (all equal probabilities), single-element softmax (degenerate).

Add torch_softmax_torch_subprocess_conformance covering interior, large-magnitude, near-uniform, and edge-case inputs along multiple dims; assert ULP-tolerant equality against scipy.special.softmax (or numpy formulation if scipy unavailable). Same for log_softmax.

Bias toward filing-from-/reality-check: this is a pre-existing instance test gap, not a new bug.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:22:44.964172677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:22:44.964172677Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-fq9","title":"Add trapezoid, cumulative_trapezoid, addr, inner to ft-api","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:31:01.289432681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:32:53.881175699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:32:53.880929192Z","close_reason":"Implemented trapezoid, cumulative_trapezoid, addr, inner with 9 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-g7f","title":"ft-data dataloader initial shuffle parity","description":"DataLoader with DataLoaderConfig::with_shuffle(true) preserves sequential order on first epoch until reset() is called. PyTorch-observable behavior expects the first epoch to be shuffled as well. Fix construction-time behavior and add regression coverage in ft-data.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:24:12.805969180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:34:13.512441665Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:34:13.512255252Z","close_reason":"Implemented first-epoch shuffle parity and regression coverage in ft-data","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
